issue_summary: Make Jupiter discovery mechanism more robust
javadoc_after:
/** *

NOTES TO DEVELOPERS

* *

Any non-private method in this class is forbidden to throw any type of * exception other than a "blacklisted exception". All other exceptions must * be caught, logged, and swallowed in order to ensure the robustness of our * discovery mechanism. * *

Discovery errors related to Unique IDs that are particular to the Jupiter * test engine should be logged at WARNING level; whereas, all other discovery * errors should be logged at DEBUG level, since the Jupiter engine has no way * of knowing that is supposed to actually handle those things (e.g., * classpath roots, packages, etc. selected by a registered discovery selector). * * @since 5.0 */
